content.full_text:
TRANSMITTAL OF CONFLICT PRCE

RE: [illegible] to FYO March 1

TO: [illegible] Letter
[illegible]

INISTRATION CONFLICT DAWN ENFORCEMENT
FOR T. DEL BOB S.
LINDA ED RICK
JUDY KEN G. BARBARA
AL FRED

EXECUTIVE LEGAL TARA
DAN KEN F. JOHN K.
MICKEY NATALIE NAN
CARLA TED GINA
JEANNE MIKE MARY ANN
JOHN G.
JAY
RICH

[illegible], Please

Take whatever action you consider appropriate

Reply direct by [illegible]

Prepare suggested reply and return to

Coordinate reply with


4/12 - RECEIVED THE LETTER. TALKED TO MICK RICHMOND. HE HAD NOT
DONE ANYTHING WITH IT.

4/12 - DUE TO THE LENGTH OF TIME SINCE THE LETTER WAS WRITTEN
BY THE NATURE OF THE LETTER, I SUGGEST THAT NO
REPLY BE MADE.

P. Harmon

2550 RAMADA WAY
CHICO, CALIFORNIA 95926

Feb. 12, 1977

Governor Edmund Brown, Jr.
Sacramento, California

Dear Governor Brown,

I'm sending this copy to you to alert your office to the
silliness and mountain of paperwork that we're being subjected
to in the name of "reform". First the candidate spends a
great deal of time attempting to file accurate reports, and then
you go back to the donors and ask for more reports. This is
government efficiency? It looks like a make-work program for
bureaucrats to me!

Sincerely,

[signature]

State of California

POLITICAL REFORM AUDIT PROGRAM
1001 G Street, Suite 200
Sacramento, CA 95814
Telephone (916) 322-3973

February 10, 1977


Quentin & Lucile Griffiths
2550 Ramada Way
Chico        CA 95926


The voters of California enacted the Political Reform Act in June
1974 election and by so doing added audits as a new dimension to the
political process. A confirmation letter, such as this, is sent to
campaign contributors, as a necessary and routine part of an audit, to
verify monetary and nonmonetary contributions reported. This proce-
dure is applied to all candidates, officials, and committees that are
subject to audit under the Act. No special significance should be
attached to either our sending, or your receiving, this confirmation
letter.

Your name is shown as a contributor on campaign statements filed by
Betty Smith for Assembly, for the period January 7, 1975 through
January 6, 1977.

Your confirmation of contributions made to the above, by furnishing
the information requested below, will assist the department in the
completion of its audit. A postage-paid envelope is enclosed for
your reply.


Political Reform Audit

Enc.

                          _______________Other_______________
Date        Check        Cash          Value*      Description
_________________________________________________________________

Sept. 10, 1976  ___________  $250.00  ___________  ___________

_________________________________________________________________

_________________________________________________________________

*If nonmonetary, enter estimated fair market value.

(If more space is needed for listing, use reverse side of this form,
or attach a separate schedule.)

sections.question_synthetic: This document is not a question-and-answer advice letter, but rather internal FPPC correspondence regarding a citizen complaint about excessive paperwork from the Political Reform Audit Program.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal transmittal/routing form, not an FPPC advice letter. Contains a citizen complaint letter and audit confirmation letter as attachments. No formal Q&A structure. Handwritten routing notes indicate it was received 4/12, discussed with Rich Sickman, and recommendation made not to reply due to time elapsed and nature of complaint. Some document text appears duplicative or fragmented in source material.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: This document is not a question-and-answer advice letter, but rather internal FPPC correspondence regarding a citizen complaint about excessive paperwork from the Political Reform Audit Program.
embedding.first_500_words:
The document is a PC TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE form, dated 4/12, addressed to Rich. The RE: field states "Discussion to FTB Audit Confirmation letter." The form lists several names under different categories such as TO, CONFLICT, ENFORCEMENT, EXECUTIVE, LEGAL, and TA&A. Names like Bob T., Linda, Judy, Dan, Mickey, Carla, Jeanne, Ken F., Natalie, Ted, Mike, Bob S., Rick, Barbara, Fred, John K., Nan, Gina, Mary Ann, John G., Jay, and Rich are present. Rich is noted as the primary recipient, with instructions to take whatever action he considers appropriate. There are checkboxes for Reply direct by 3-8-77, Prepare suggested reply and return to [blank], and Coordinate reply with [blank]. Handwritten notes at the bottom of the page state: 4/12 - Received the letter, talked to Rich Sickman, he has not done anything with it. 4/13 - Due to the length of time since the letter was written and the nature of the letter, I suggest that no reply be made. A. Henderson Feb. 12, 1977 Governor Edmund Brown, Jr. Sacramento, California Dear Governor Brown, I'm sending this copy to you to alert your office to the silliness and mountain of paperwork that we're being subjected to in the name of "reform". First the candidate spends a great deal of time attempting to file accurate reports, and then you go back to the donors and ask for more reports. This is government efficiency? It looks like a make-work program for bureaucrats to me! Sincerely, [signature] I resent this needless paperwork in the name of "reform." This contribution has been reported by the candidate, it's been spelled out in the local press, and now we waste more money and time checking up on it! State of California POLITICAL REFORM AUDIT PROGRAM 1001 G Street, Suite 200 Sacramento, CA 95814 Telephone (916) 322-3973 February 10, 1977 Quentin & Lucile Griffiths 2550 Ramada Way Chico CA 95926 The voters of California enacted the Political Reform Act in the June 1974 election and by so doing added audits as a new dimension to the political process. A confirmation letter, such as this, is sent to campaign contributors, as a necessary and routine part of an audit, to verify monetary and nonmonetary contributions reported. This procedure is applied to all candidates, officials, and committees that are subject to audit under the Act. No special significance should be attached to either our sending, or your receiving, this confirmation letter. Your name is shown as a contributor on campaign statements filed by Betty Smith for Assembly, for the period January 7, 1975 through January 6, 1977. Your confirmation of contributions made to the above, by furnishing the information requested below, will assist the department in the completion of its audit. A postage-paid envelope is enclosed for your reply. Political Reform Audit Enc.
Date Check Cash Value* Description
Sept. 10, 1976 ✓ $250.00
*If nonmonetary, enter estimated fair market value. Signature: Lucile Griffiths Date: Feb. 11, 1977 (If more space
embedding.summary: Internal FPPC transmittal form routing a complaint from a campaign contributor objecting to the Political Reform Audit Program's confirmation letter process as unnecessary paperwork and government inefficiency, dated February 1977.
